content: This letter was written by Yudas the younger brother of Yacob. (Note that he wasnât the Yudas who handed Yeshua over to the Jewish religious leaders. Note also that Yudas is mistakenly called Yude in most English Bibles, and Yacob is quite wrongly called James in most English Bibles.)

We donât know which group of believers that this letter was sent to. Here in this letter, Yudas warns the believers about teachers of false doctrines who teach that itâs okay for believers to continue sinning since theyâll just be forgiven due to Godâs mercy. Thatâs the reason Yudas wrote this, to warn the believers so that they wonât fall for false beliefs, but instead will continue to follow the true teaching that came from God. He also encourages the readers that itâs necessary to strive to continue in their faith in God along with turning away from evil actions.
The work and judgement of false teachers
